meta.title: The Last Broadcast
meta.premise: A true-crime podcaster is found dead hours before her final episode drops. The recording still holds the key.
meta.tone: modern media thriller, neon-and-static, sharp dialogue
meta.artStyle: Modern media noir portrait, head-and-shoulders, studio purple and LED blue palette, slight film grain, contemporary clothing, same cool podcast-studio lighting for every cast member, mystery game NPC portrait
meta.setting: A late-night podcast studio complex. Studio B, control booth, green room. ON AIR is dark.
meta.summary: Host Lena Ortiz is dead in Studio B hours before Blood Signal was supposed to air — headphones still on, the main session wiped, a backup waveform that does not match the official story. On site: producer Kai Okonkwo, engineer Amy Chen, rival host Blake Mercer, intern Riley Cho, and network exec Dana Ruiz. Someone turned a final episode into a crime scene. The feed will not wait forever for the truth.
meta.theMystery: Who killed Lena Ortiz in Studio B before her last episode — and what in that show was worth killing to bury?

player.role: Independent investigator brought in before the network freezes the scene
player.appearance: Unremarkable on purpose — someone who can stand in a green room without looking like press
player.clothing: Dark jacket, soft shoes, badge lanyard that is not network security
player.background: Hired quietly when a death hits a production floor and legal wants a clean private record before police narrative takes over.
player.publicPerception: Not staff, not quite cops. People decide whether to help based on fear of the network.
player.performanceNotes: Studio people may call you 'Investigator' or avoid names. You do not run the feed.
player.startingKnowledge: Host Lena Ortiz is dead in Studio B. Final episode 'Blood Signal' was due tonight. On site: engineer Kai Okonkwo, guest researcher Dr. Amy Chen, rival host Blake Mercer, intern Riley Cho, building security Dana Ruiz. The raw session file is missing from the primary drive.
player.objective: Search the studio, recover what the recording still holds, pressure the cast and crew, and accuse the murderer with method and motive.

openingNarration: ON AIR light is dark. Studio B smells like coffee and ozone. Lena Ortiz is slumped over the boom arm, headphones still on. The waveform on the backup recorder is a jagged confession the main file no longer holds. Kai stands in the booth doorway like someone who has already aged a year. Down the hall: a rival host who should not have badge access, an engineer who knows every cable, an intern who saw too much, and a network executive already thinking about press. Lena’s final episode dies with her unless you find who killed her — and what story they were trying to kill with her.

wrapUp.performanceNotes: Podcast-world aftermath — statements, deleted episodes, quiet studio. RETURN TO NORMAL: Blake held, story no longer brand-controlled, truth can air.

locations.name: Studio B floor
locations.description: Foam walls, mics, a body at the desk. Hall to booth, green room, and server closet.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Strangled with her own headphone cable. Defensive marks. Backup handheld recorder still rolling in her bag — not the main DAW.
Session file BloodSignal_FINAL deleted at 8:14 p.m. Deletion used Kai's login — or someone who had it.

locations.name: Control booth
locations.description: Glass view into Studio B. Faders, talkback, coffee rings.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Talkback engaged at 8:09 from booth to studio — one-way. Someone spoke to Lena without entering. Badge log can show who was in the booth.
Printout of Lena's unreleased outline: 'Mercer killed the story — and maybe more.' Blake's show logo in the corner as if he annotated it.

locations.name: Server closet
locations.description: Humming racks. Badge reader. A USB stick taped under a shelf.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Audio clip: Lena confronting someone off-mic — 'You leaked my source to bury the story. I air it tonight.' A man's voice: Blake's cadence. Then scuffle.
Blake Mercer badge in control booth 8:08–8:16. Kai's badge still in studio. Riley in green room. Dana at desk.

characters.name: Lena Ortiz
characters.shortBio: True-crime host of Blood Signal — found dead in Studio B hours before air.

knowledge.public: Deceased. Found in Studio B with headphones still on.

characters.name: Kai Okonkwo
characters.shortBio: Studio engineer; last login on the DAW.

knowledge.public: Kai was balancing levels, stepped out for cables, came back to Lena dead. Login was unlocked — bad habit.

private.content: Kai saw Blake slip into the control booth alone near 8:10.

defenses: Easy false suspect because of login

characters.name: Blake Mercer
characters.shortBio: Rival true-crime host; smile like a brand.

knowledge.public: Blake says he waited in the green room for a joint promo. He never entered Studio B.

private.content: He admits he used the booth to 'listen in' — not to kill. The USB will break that.

knowledge.public: She was reviewing notes in the green room. She heard raised voices through the wall around 8:10.

characters.name: Riley Cho
characters.shortBio: Intern; first death on a job.

knowledge.public: Riley fetched water, saw Blake leave the green room toward the booth hall.
Badge logs are in the server closet. No forced entry from outside.

evidence.name: Backup handheld recorder
evidence.description: Waveform spike then silence at 00:41:12 into the episode clock.

evidence.name: Stolen episode outline
evidence.description: Lena's outline accusing Mercer of killing a story; in Blake's bag.

evidence.name: USB confrontation audio
evidence.description: Lena vs Blake off-mic; scuffle after threat to air the story.

beats.narrationHints: Murder, not an accident with gear.
The episode already recorded its own murder.
Blake's brand cracks. Finish before he controls the narrative.
Blaming the engineer freezes the crew.
Blake walks with lawyers and a podcast denial. Failure.
Studio aftermath. The show will not air as planned — something truer will.

solution.summary: Blake Mercer entered the control booth, confronted Lena about her episode exposing him for leaking a source, then entered Studio B and strangled her with the headphone cable. He deleted the main session under Kai's open login and hid a USB of the confrontation. Badge logs and audio prove the timeline.
solution.method: Strangulation with headphone cable after booth approach
solution.motive: Stop Lena airing evidence he killed a story / leaked a source

endings.title: End of show
endings.templateNotes: Blake is held. The USB and outline close the loop. Kai grieves. RETURN TO NORMAL: counsel and platform process take the case; the feed will carry a different final episode — truth, not brand.

endings.title: Cold open
endings.templateNotes: You name Blake before every file is checked. He still folds. RETURN TO NORMAL: he is detained; the narrative is no longer his alone. Do not invent unfound audio.

endings.title: Pending review
endings.templateNotes: Enough to detain; lawyers already circling. Thin RETURN TO NORMAL: the immediate cover story is broken; the full public cut is incomplete.

endings.title: Bad take
endings.templateNotes: Wrong name on the record. The real killer owns the narrative by morning.

endings.title: He walked
endings.templateNotes: Blake leaves with counsel and a denial episode. Lena's last broadcast never airs clean.
